signals.headline: Ethereum ETFs Post Strong Weekly Inflows | YES | HIGH confidence**
signals.raw:
Ethereum ETFs Post Strong Weekly Inflows | YES | HIGH confidence**
Ethereum ETFs logged $276 million in inflows for the week, with Fidelity's FETH leading at $126 million.[2] Ethereum spot ETFs also recorded their 8th consecutive day of net inflows at $67.77 million on April 20.[4]

signals.headline: Based on the search results provided, here are the key market-moving developments:
signals.raw: Based on the search results provided, here are the key market-moving developments:

signals.headline: BlackRock $900M Bitcoin Purchase Boosts $80K Odds | YES | HIGH confidence**
signals.raw:
BlackRock $900M Bitcoin Purchase Boosts $80K Odds | YES | HIGH confidence**
BlackRock purchased $900 million in Bitcoin through its iShares Bitcoin Trust (IBIT) ETF, causing the Polymarket contract for Bitcoin reaching $80,000 in April to jump to 71.5% YES, up from 44% just 24 hours ago.[1]

signals.headline: Spot Bitcoin ETFs Extend 5-Day Inflow Streak | YES | HIGH confidence**
signals.raw:
Spot Bitcoin ETFs Extend 5-Day Inflow Streak | YES | HIGH confidence**
U.S. spot Bitcoin ETFs recorded $238.37 million in net inflows on April 20, extending an institutional inflow streak to five consecutive sessions—the strongest tempo since February.[2] BlackRock's IBIT led with $256.05 million in inflows on that day.[2]

signals.headline: Bitcoin Trading Near $75,900 | YES | HIGH confidence**
signals.raw:
Bitcoin Trading Near $75,900 | YES | HIGH confidence**
Bitcoin is trading around $75,900, up over 2% in the last 24 hours, with the total crypto market cap reaching approximately $2.5 trillion.[3] This represents continued momentum toward the $80,000 target, though still below that level as of the latest data points provided.

signals.headline: US–Iran ceasefire extended (April 22, 2026)** | YES | HIGH confidence[3]
signals.raw:
US–Iran ceasefire extended (April 22, 2026)** | YES | HIGH confidence[3]

Goldman Sachs' U.S. Inflation Monitor (April 20, 2026) confirms headline CPI surged to 3.29% y/y in March due to Iran war-driven energy prices (oil from $71 to $103/barrel), but core inflation is decelerating toward 2.2% (excluding tariffs), with forecasts for core PCE easing to enable Fed rate cuts later in 2026[1].
